Financial Performance

Step-by-step savings calculation from historical benchmark to earned savings/losses.

Step Description Value MSSP Median Peer Median
1 Assigned Beneficiaries 7,972 p23 13,159 8,339
2 Historical Benchmark (per capita) $12,250 p68 $11,439 $11,243
3 Regional Adjustment +$180
4 Prior Savings Adjustment +-
5 Adjustment Category Regional Adjustment
6 Updated Benchmark (per capita) $15,211 p84 $13,279 $13,033
7 Total Benchmark (all beneficiaries) $118,939,154
8 Total Expenditure (all beneficiaries) $109,147,439
9 Per Capita Expenditure (PY) $13,959 p77 $12,693 $12,777
10 Generated Savings / Losses $9,791,715 $6,931,306 —
11 Savings Rate 8.23% p82 4.21% 3.20%
12 Minimum Savings Rate (threshold) 3.21%
13 Guardrail Applied —
14 Final Shared Savings Rate 40.00%
15 Final Shared Loss Rate -
16 Earned Savings / Losses $3,838,352 $3,685,876 —
Revenue-to-Expense Category Low Revenue

Per Capita Expenditure by Population Segment

Annual per-beneficiary expenditure across benchmark years (BY1–BY3) and the performance year (PY). Includes Parts A and B FFS spending.

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Risk-Adj. Expected Difference
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) $88,023 $97,465 $103,667 $114,926 ↑ $95,266 +$19,660
Disabled $10,194 $11,285 $10,229 $12,755 ↑ $11,906 +$849
Aged, Dual-Eligible $16,133 $19,493 $20,449 $21,413 ↑ $18,215 +$3,198
Aged, Non-Dual $10,278 $10,698 $10,843 $13,100 ↑ $12,216 +$884
Total $13,959 $12,693 $12,777

Risk Scores

Hierarchical Condition Category risk scores. A score of 1.0 = national average. Below 1.0 means the population is healthier than average.

CMS-HCC Risk Scores

Population Segment BY1BY2BY3PY Trend Coding Gap MSSP Med.
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) 1.035 1.033 1.112 1.020 → +0.009 0.967
Disabled 0.952 0.937 0.943 0.986 ↑ -0.008 0.934
Aged, Dual-Eligible 1.044 1.045 1.068 0.962 ↓ -0.085 0.954
Aged, Non-Dual 1.013 1.007 1.011 1.013 → -0.025 0.983

Demographic Risk Scores

Demographic-only risk scores (age/sex/Medicaid status). Compared with HCC risk scores, the difference reflects clinical coding intensity.

Population Segment BY3PY
End-Stage Renal Disease (ESRD) — 1.011
Disabled — 0.994
Aged, Dual-Eligible — 1.047
Aged, Non-Dual — 1.038

Risk Redistribution Weights (PY)

Fraction of the ACO's total risk attributable to each population segment.

ESRD Weight 3.6%
Disabled Weight 4.8%
Aged Dual Weight 9.2%
Aged Non-Dual Weight 82.4%

MSSP quality measures. Measures marked "lower is better" are inverted — a lower score indicates better performance.

Utilization — Admissions & Visits Rehab Admissions (per 1,000) 14 15 ADM_Rehab
Utilization — Admissions & Visits Short-Term Acute Admissions (per 1,000) 226 229 ADM_S_Trm
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Admissions (per 1,000) 47 40 P_SNF_ADM
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Average Length of Stay (days) 30 27 p77 SNF_LOS
Utilization — Admissions & Visits SNF Average Payment per Stay $22,881 $15,338 p90 SNF_PayperStay
Methodology

Data source: MSSP ACO Performance PY2024 (dataset ID: mssp-performance). Vintage: PY2024. Downloaded: 2026-04-13. Rows in source: 476.

MSSP ACO Performance reports financial and quality performance for Accountable Care Organizations participating in the Medicare Shared Savings Program. Savings/losses are calculated against risk-adjusted, regionally-weighted benchmarks.

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
